figures.caption: Figure 2: Jaccard distance between SWA heads selected by seven methods for ρ = 0.25 (upper plot) and ρ = 0.5 (lower plot). The distances for 8B and 14B models are shown in the lower and upper triangles, respectively.

Table 15: Detailed continual pretraining performances on the NIAH benchmark for 4 SWA hybridization methods for 4 Qwen3 models (1.7B, 8B, 14B, 30B) under SWA ratio ρ=0.75. The highest score under each configuration is highlighted in bold.

Table 18: Detailed zero-shot performances on the LongBench benchmark for 4 SWA hybridization methods for 4 Qwen3 models (1.7B, 8B, 14B, 30B) under SWA ratio ρ=0.75 when SWA windows size is {256, 512, 2048, 4096}. The highest score under each configuration is highlighted in bold.

Table 19: Zero-shot scores on the NIAH benchmark for BOSCH when using A′ ρl across 4 Qwen3 models under 3 SWA ratios ρ ∈ {0.5, 0.75, 0.875}. The left side of→ corresponds to ρs, while the right side corresponds to ρl. A′ ρl indicates results obtained after replacing a randomly selected subset of heads at a given configuration (e.g., ρl=0.75) with heads that did not appear in a smaller configuration (e.g., ρs=0.5). Scores are averaged over three runs with different seeds.

Figure 4: NIAH (first 2 plots) and LongBench (last 2 plots) zero-shot average performances (y-axis) for Qwen3-8BBase and Qwen3-14B-Base models as a function of varying the SWA window size (x-axis). We report scores for two layer-level and two head-level SWA hybridization methods using ρ=0.75. The performance of the full-attention original models (without additional training) is shown as a straight line with no markers.

Figure 5: Latency and memory statistics comparing the original Qwen3-8B-Base model with SWA hybrid variants at different SWA ρ ratios using BOSCH. The left plot shows prefill throughput (tokens/s), the middle plot shows decoding throughput in the same units, and the right plot shows p90 memory allocation (GB). These statistics are measured with input prompts of lengths ranging from 64 to 1M tokens (x-axis). Missing data points correspond to runs where the model encountered out-of-memory (OOM) error.
Figure 6: Latency and memory statistics of BOSCH ρ=0.75 Qwen3-8B-Base SWA hybrid models when varying the SWA window size between 256 and 4096. Notation is the same as in Figure 5.

Figure 1: Illustrative application of BOSCH to a Transformer with L=8 layers and H=8 heads per layer, targeting ρ=0.5% SWA heads. Left (Stage 1: layer-importance detection): each row is a layer; blue squares are selfattention heads (z=1), red squares are SWA heads (z=0); light-red squares with a loupe indicate the layer(s) currently scored via black-box optimization, from top to bottom. Middle (Stage 2: adaptive ratio assignment): for each layer ℓ we compute the relative drop δℓ (from the original model), normalize it to an importance weight wℓ∈ [0, 1], and map it to a per-layer SWA ratio rℓ while respecting the global budget. Right (Stage 3: multi-layer head selection under adaptive ratios): layers are bucketed by ratio into groups gℓ and optimized jointly from more SWA-tolerant to less tolerant; the example highlights searching layers with gℓ=2 using rℓ=0.25, yielding the final head mask z. "−" indicates that either entire layer is full-attention or SWA ( no optimization is applied).

Figure 8: Jaccard distance between SWA heads selected by seven methods for ρ ∈ {0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.875}. The distances for Qwen3-1.7B-Base and Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base models are shown in the lower and upper triangles, respectively.

Figure 3: NIAH (first 2 plots) and LongBench (last 2 plots) performances (y-axis) for Qwen3-8B-Base and Qwen314B-Base models as a function of intermediate checkpoints during continual pretraining on 2.5B tokens (x-axis). We report scores for two layer-level and two head-level SWA hybridization methods using ρ=0.75. The performance of the full-attention original models (without additional training) is shown as a straight line with no markers.

Figure 7: Jaccard distance between SWA heads selected by seven methods for ρ ∈ {0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.875}. The distances for Qwen3-8B-Base and Qwen3-14B-Base models are shown in the lower and upper triangles, respectively.

Table 20: Zero-shot scores on the LongBench benchmark for BOSCH when using A′ ρl across 4 Qwen3 models under 3 SWA ratios ρ ∈ {0.5, 0.75, 0.875} (right side of →). A′ ρl indicates results obtained after replacing a randomly selected subset of heads at a given configuration (e.g., ρl = 0.75) with heads that did not appear in a smaller configuration (e.g., ρs=0.5). Scores are averaged over three runs with different seeds.

Table 5: Zero-shot average scores on the NIAH and LongBench benchmarks for BOSCH across 4 Qwen3 models under 3 SWA ratios ρl ∈ {0.5, 0.75, 0.875}. The left side of→ corresponds to ρs, while the right side corresponds to ρl. A′ ρl indicates results obtained after replacing a randomly selected subset of heads at a given configuration (e.g., ρl=0.75) with heads that did not appear in a smaller configuration (e.g., ρs=0.5). Results in theA′ ρl columns are averaged over 3 runs with different seeds. The Aρl column shows the results for the originally selected SWA heads set.

Table 6: Detailed zero-shot performance on the NIAH benchmark at 64k and 128k for the original models and four SWA hybridization methods (with SWA ratio ρ = 0.75) applied to four Qwen3 models (1.7B, 8B, 14B, 30B). Zero-shot length extrapolation is performed using a YaRN factor of 2 and 4 for 64k and 128k, respectively. The Avg. block reports, for each model, the mean of its single and multikey scores. The highest score under each configuration is highlighted in bold.

Table 1: Zero-shot average scores on the NIAH benchmark for SWA hybridization methods across 4 Qwen3 models (1.7B-30B) under 4 SWA ρ ratios. The highest and second-highest scores for each model under each ratio are highlighted in bold and underline, respectively. *For readability purposes, we repeat the original model’s performance in each column (SWA hybridization is not applied to the original model).
Table 2: Zero-shot average scores on the LongBench benchmark for SWA hybridization methods across 4 Qwen3 models under 4 SWA ρ ratios. Notations follow those used in Table 1.
